---
title: "Lecture 14 Notes: Perception II - Camera Modeling and Calibration"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 14 slides](../slides/04-perception-2-rgbd-object-pose-estimation.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Camera models convert 3D geometry to 2D image observations.
- Calibration identifies intrinsics/extrinsics and lens distortion parameters.
- Reprojection error is a practical metric for calibration quality.

## Pinhole projection

\[
\begin{bmatrix}
u \\ v \\ 1
\end{bmatrix}
\sim
K [R \mid t]
\begin{bmatrix}
X \\ Y \\ Z \\ 1
\end{bmatrix}
\]

## Review questions

1. What is the difference between intrinsic and extrinsic parameters?
2. How does poor calibration affect 3D perception pipelines?
3. Why are multiple views needed for robust calibration?
